column9: Active learning is a teaching approach that actively engages students in the learning process rather than passively receiving information. It emphasizes meaningful participation, collaboration, exploration, and reflection. The goal is to develop critical thinking, creativity, and problem-solving skills through hands-on experiences rather than rote memorization.&lt

column7: Projects and activities allow children to construct knowledge through doing and reflecting.&lt
Group discussions promote social learning, communication skills, and the sharing of diverse perspectives.&lt
Real-life problem-solving connects classroom learning with the outside world, making education more relevant and practical.&lt
Together, these approaches ensure deeper understanding and longer retention of concepts.&lt

Rote memorization of facts, is a passive method where learners simply recall information without necessarily understanding or applying it. This approach does not encourage active participation or critical engagement and is therefore not a feature of active learning.&lt

column9: Attribution theory explores how individuals explain the causes of their successes and failures. These explanations influence motivation, effort, and future performance.&lt

Attributing both successes and failures to internal factors, such as effort or ability, encourages students to take responsibility for their learning. When success is seen as a result of their own actions, students feel motivated and confident.&lt

column7: Similarly, viewing failures as linked to controllable internal causes fosters a growth mindset, leading them to try harder and adopt strategies to improve.&lt
This attribution pattern supports sustained effort and better performance by emphasizing personal control and accountability.&lt

column9: Attributing both successes and failures to external factors can lead to helplessness and reduced motivation since students may feel outcomes are beyond their control.&lt

column7: Attributing success to internal causes but failure to external causes may reduce the willingness to learn from mistakes and grow.&lt
Attributing success to external causes and failure to internal causes can lower self-esteem and discourage effort, as failures are seen as personal shortcomings.&lt

column9: the correct answer is attributing both successes and failures to internal factors.&lt
column19: आरोपण सिद्धांत यह पता लगाता है कि व्यक्ति अपनी सफलताओं और असफलताओं के कारणों की व्याख्या कैसे करते हैं। ये व्याख्याएँ अभिप्रेरणा, प्रयास और भविष्य के प्रदर्शन को प्रभावित करती हैं।&lt

column7: सफलताओं और असफलताओं दोनों को आंतरिक कारकों, जैसे प्रयास या क्षमता, के लिए आरोपित करना, छात्रों को अपने सीखने की जिम्मेदारी लेने के लिए प्रोत्साहित करता है। जब सफलता को अपने कार्यों का परिणाम माना जाता है, तो छात्र प्रेरित और आत्मविश्वास महसूस करते हैं।&lt

column3: This principle highlights the uniqueness of each individual and acknowledges that development varies from person to person due to genetic, environmental, and experiential factors.&amp
The principle of individual differences is fundamental because it recognizes that no two individuals develop in the same way or at the same rate.&lt
These differences can be influenced by a range of factors, including genetic predispositions, cultural context, family environment, and personal experiences.&lt
By acknowledging these differences, psychologists can better understand and appreciate the complexity of human development.&lt

This principle is crucial for tailoring educational and therapeutic approaches. In educational settings, understanding individual differences allows educators to create more personalized learning experiences that cater to the unique needs, strengths, and weaknesses of each student.&lt
Recognizing these differences can lead to more effective teaching strategies and improved learning outcomes.&lt

column5: density of 46 persons per square kilometre,&amp

column3: This is the lowest among all the Union Territories in India.&lt
This low population density can be attributed to the fact that the Andaman and Nicobar Islands are remote and composed of around 572 islands, islets and rocks, some of which are uninhabitable.&lt
The difficult terrain and the remote location contribute to the low population density.&lt
